description: Oracle test facts for convergent solutions between biological systems and AI/RL algorithms. Tests knowledge of deep computational equivalences, not surface analogies.

facts.context: E. coli chemotaxis (run-and-tumble with temporal gradient sensing) is computationally equivalent to:
facts.truth: stochastic gradient descent with momentum via sensory adaptation
distractors: deterministic hill climbing without any noise component
random walk with no directional bias whatsoever
exhaustive grid search over the concentration field

facts.context: Bacterial chemotaxis achieves perfect adaptation (return to prestimulus baseline) through:
facts.truth: integral feedback — methylation integrates signal over time
distractors: proportional feedback — direct receptor sensitivity scaling
derivative feedback — sensing rate of concentration change
feedforward prediction — anticipating future concentrations

facts.context: Schultz (1997) showed that midbrain dopamine neuron firing encodes:
facts.truth: temporal difference error δ = r + γV(s') - V(s)
distractors: absolute reward magnitude at the current timestep
cumulative discounted return from episode start
policy gradient ∇log π(a|s) weighted by advantage

facts.context: The basal ganglia direct pathway (D1 striatal neurons → GPi inhibition → thalamus) implements:
facts.truth: the actor's Go signal — facilitating selected actions
distractors: the critic's value estimate for current state
the replay buffer storing past state transitions
the exploration noise added to action selection

facts.context: C. elegans switches between local area-restricted search and long-range roaming. This maps to:
distractors: pure exploitation with no exploratory component
model-based planning with full environment simulation
imitation learning from conspecific trajectories

facts.context: Levy flight foraging patterns (heavy-tailed step distributions) are optimal when:
facts.truth: targets are sparse and randomly distributed (destructive foraging)
distractors: targets are densely packed in known spatial clusters
the environment has no spatial structure whatsoever
the forager has a complete map of all target locations

facts.context: Hebbian learning (Δw ∝ pre × post) differs from backpropagation primarily because:
facts.truth: Hebbian uses only local information — no global error signal needed
distractors: Hebbian requires labeled training data for each synapse
Hebbian computes exact gradients of a global loss function
Hebbian needs full network topology to update each weight

facts.context: Hippocampal grid cells and place cells solve the same computational problem as:
facts.truth: SLAM — simultaneous localization and mapping from noisy sensors
distractors: convex optimization over a known fixed cost landscape
batch supervised classification of spatial categories
lossless compression of visual input streams to memory

facts.context: Bacterial quorum sensing (collective behavioral switch at signal threshold) is analogous to:
distractors: centralized master-slave replication with single leader
synchronous lockstep execution across all participants
client-server request-response with guaranteed ordering

facts.context: The cerebellum's forward model of body dynamics is computationally equivalent to:
facts.truth: model predictive control — predicting outcomes to enable smooth action
distractors: purely reactive proportional-derivative feedback control
tabular Q-learning with discrete state-action lookup tables
genetic algorithm search over motor program populations

facts.context: Ant pheromone trail optimization converges to short paths because:
facts.truth: shorter paths get reinforced faster — positive feedback via stigmergy
distractors: ants explicitly compute shortest path using graph algorithms
pheromone evaporation is slower on geometrically longer paths
each ant carries a complete map of the explored territory

facts.context: Synaptic eligibility traces in neuroscience solve the same problem as:
facts.truth: temporal credit assignment — bridging the delay between action and reward
distractors: spatial credit assignment across parallel network layers
input normalization to stabilize gradient magnitudes
weight regularization to prevent catastrophic forgetting

facts.context: The C. elegans escape response (reversal + omega turn triggered by harsh touch) maps to:
facts.truth: interrupt-driven reactive architecture — stimulus triggers fixed action pattern
distractors: deliberative planning with multi-step lookahead search
model-free policy gradient optimization over escape routes
hierarchical reinforcement learning with subgoal decomposition

facts.context: The drift-diffusion model of perceptual decision-making is mathematically equivalent to:
facts.truth: sequential probability ratio test (SPRT) — optimal sequential hypothesis testing
distractors: maximum likelihood estimation from a fixed sample batch
Bayesian model comparison with exhaustive prior enumeration
kernel density estimation over the stimulus feature space

facts.context: When evolution and gradient descent converge on the same solution (e.g., dopamine=TD error), this suggests:
facts.truth: fundamental computational constraints — both found the same optimum
distractors: pure coincidence with no deeper computational explanation
AI researchers deliberately copied the biological mechanism
the biological solution is always suboptimal compared to AI

facts.context: Swarm consensus via neighbor averaging converges to the population mean because:
facts.truth: iterated averaging on a connected graph contracts to the dominant eigenvector
distractors: each agent independently computes the global mean directly
a central coordinator broadcasts the correct answer to agents
agents randomly guess values until they happen to all match
